headerDisplay: Transform your HomePod mini into a smart music sensor for HomeKit. Create powerful automations based on what's playing on your HomePods. **New in v1.4.6:** Stereo Pair Support! Create a single sensor for HomePod pairs.

detectMusic.title: 🎵 Detect Music
detectMusic.description: Trigger sensor when music is playing (Apple Music, Spotify, etc.)

detectPodcasts.title: 🎙️ Detect Podcasts
detectPodcasts.description: Trigger sensor when podcasts or audiobooks are playing

detectMovies.title: 🎬 Detect Movies/TV
detectMovies.description: Trigger sensor when movies or TV shows are playing via AirPlay

maxDuration.title: ⏱️ Max Duration (seconds)
maxDuration.description: Maximum duration of content to detect (filters out long content like movies). Default: 600 seconds (10 minutes)

requireArtist.title: 🎤 Require Artist Field
requireArtist.description: Only detect content that has artist metadata (helps filter out non-music content)

updateInterval.title: 🔄 Update Interval (seconds)
updateInterval.description: How often to check HomePod status. Lower = faster updates but more CPU usage

name.title: Display Name
name.description: Friendly name for this HomePod (e.g., 'Bedroom', 'Kitchen'). For stereo pairs, use the same name for both HomePods.

deviceId.title: Device ID
deviceId.description: HomePod identifier from scan (remove colons/dashes). Example: A294FB11E039. Run 'python3 -m pyatv.scripts.atvremote scan' to find IDs.

stereoPair.title: 🎵 Part of Stereo Pair
stereoPair.description: Enable if this HomePod is part of a stereo pair. Give both HomePods the same name to create a single sensor that monitors both.
